content.body: DNA replication copies the genetic code before cell division. Gene expression converts the DNA code into proteins through transcription (DNA→mRNA) and translation (mRNA→protein).

Central Dogma: DNA → RNA → Protein

Transcription: DNA template → mRNA (in nucleus)
Translation: mRNA → protein (at ribosome)

Codon: 3 mRNA bases = 1 amino acid
64 codons code for 20 amino acids (genetic code is degenerate)

content.title: Transcription Example
content.body:
DNA template: 3'-TAC-CGT-AAA-5'
mRNA produced: 5'-AUG-GCA-UUU-3'
(A pairs with U in RNA; T pairs with A; G pairs with C)
AUG = Start codon (Met)
GCA = Alanine
UUU = Phenylalanine

questions.question: Transcription produces:
questions.explanation: Transcription reads the DNA template to produce messenger RNA (mRNA)

questions.question: Translation occurs at the:
questions.explanation: Ribosomes read mRNA codons and assemble amino acids into proteins

questions.question: How many mRNA bases make one codon?
questions.explanation: A codon is a triplet of 3 RNA bases that codes for one amino acid

questions.question: The start codon that begins translation is:
questions.explanation: AUG is the universal start codon, coding for methionine

questions.question: DNA replication is called semi-conservative because:
options: Only half the DNA is copied
Each new double helix has one old and one new strand
It happens in half the time
questions.answer: Each new double helix has one old and one new strand
questions.explanation: Each new DNA molecule retains one original strand — semi-conservative replication
